channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Voters in California and Nevada consider ban on forced labor aimed at protecting prisoners
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California and Nevada voters will decide in November whether to ban forced prison labor by removing language from their state constitutions rooted in the legacy of chattel slavery.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN and RIO YAMAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Alex Murdaugh settles South Carolina lawsuit with Mallory Beach family for 2019 boat crash
item.description: South Carolina Judge Daniel Hall has ordered disgraced attorney-turned-convicted murderer Alex Murdaugh to pay a $500,000 settlement to the family of Mallory Beach.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jets acquire Raiders wide receiver Davante Adams, AP sources say
item.description: The New York Jets have agreed to terms to acquire disgruntled wide receiver Davante Adams from the Las Vegas Raiders, reuniting him with quarterback Aaron Rodgers, two people with knowledge of the deal told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Pandas arrive in the US. Next stop is the National Zoo
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The National Zoo's long dark panda drought is coming to an end. Eleven months after the zoo sent its three wildly popular pandas — Mei Xiang, Tian Tian and their cub Xiao Qi Ji — back to China, a new pair of bears arrived in the United States on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: ASHRAF KHALIL and DIDI TANG, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: North Carolina governor candidate Mark Robinson sues CNN over report about posts on porn site
item.description: RALEIGH, N.C. (AP) — North Carolina Lt. Gov. Mark Robinson, a Republican, sued CNN on Tuesday over its recent report that he made explicit racial and sexual posts on a pornography website’s message board, calling the reporting reckless and defamatory.
item.dc:creator: GARY D. ROBERTSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Al Roker and his oldest daughter, Courtney, team up on a cookbook that celebrates their family
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Al Roker remembers the moment when it became clear to him that his oldest daughter was an honest-to-goodness chef. “We were talking, and she was in the kitchen, and she’s looking at me, but she’s chiffonading these herbs and not looking down," he recalled recently.
item.dc:creator: MARK KENNEDY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Man allegedly killed by son,19, in California was renowned photojournalist
item.description: In a bizarre twist to a grisly crime on a popular hiking trail in the San Gabriel Mountains where a 19-year-old man allegedly stabbed his father to death, the victim has been identified as a well-known, award-winning war photographer, photojournalist and professor at the University of the Arts Londo
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KREX 5

item.title: Petitions for union representation doubled under Biden's presidency, first increase since 1970s
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There has been a doubling of petitions by workers to have union representation during President Joe Biden's administration, according to figures released Tuesday by the National Labor Relations Board.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Supreme Court opens door to Texas online journalist's lawsuit over her 2017 arrest
item.description: The Supreme Court has ordered a federal appeals court to take a new look at the lawsuit filed by a Texas-based online citizen journalist who said she was wrongly arrested in a case that drew attention from national media organizations and free speech advocates.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Suspected 'murder hornet' found in Washington State
item.description: The northern giant hornet, sometimes nicknamed the "murder hornet," has been known to attack and kill entire honeybee hives.
item.dc:creator: John Ross Ferrara, KRON4

item.title: Taylor Swift shows prompt earlier closure of I-65 in Indianapolis
item.description: The initial dates would have overlapped with the three Indianapolis dates on Taylor Swift's Eras Tour.
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Survivors and relatives in Maine's deadliest shooting start the process of suing the Army
item.description: LEWISTON, Maine (AP) — Lawyers for 100 survivors and relatives of victims of the deadliest shooting in Maine history have begun the formal process of suing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: PATRICK WHITTLE and DAVID SHARP, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Massive meat recall includes hundreds of products sold at Walmart, Target, more
item.description: Check your fridge and freezer: A massive meat recall linked to possible listeria contamination has impacted hundreds of ready-to-eat meals sold at major grocery store chains across the U.S., including Walmart, Trader Joe's and Target.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, Fox 5

item.title: Convicted killer Susan Smith charged after speaking with filmmaker
item.description: GREENWOOD, S.C. (WSPA) - Convicted killer Susan Smith has been convicted of a disciplinary charge weeks before her parole hearing. Smith, of Union County, was sentenced to life in prison for the murder of her children in Oct. 1994. The SC Department of Corrections confirmed with 7NEWS that on Aug.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, WGN 9

item.title: In Missouri, Halloween night signs were required at the homes of sex offenders. Until now
item.description: Many states have laws designed to keep children away from convicted sexual offenders on Halloween night, such as curfews for those on offender registries and requirements to keep their porch lights off.
item.dc:creator: Jim Salter, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Walgreens to close 1,200 US stores
item.description: The plan announced Tuesday includes the closing of 300 stores that had been approved under a previous cost-cutting plan.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Geraldo Rivera backing Harris: Trump 'cannot be trusted to honor the Constitution'
item.description: Television personality Geraldo Rivera revealed Monday that he is backing Vice President Kamala Harris over former President Donald Trump in the election, now less than a month away.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden responds to hurricane survivors' fury over the '$750' FEMA payment and more top headlines
item.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Butler rally shooting survivors blame Trump assassination attempt on Secret Service: 'Rush job'
item.description: The two men that survived the assassination attempt on former President Trump during a rally in Butler, Pennsylvania, are blaming the U.S. Secret Service for its failure to prevent it.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump edges out Harris with early voters in battleground states
item.description: Former President Trump has a slight edge over Vice President Harris with early voters in battleground states, new polling shows, a promising sign for the Republican in the highly competitive presidential race.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Rep. Marie Gluesenkamp Perez, challenger Joe Kent face off in Washington 3rd District debate
item.description: One of the races that could determine the fate of Congress this election season happens to be the rematch race for Washington's 3rd District, and on Monday night, the candidates will debate on KOIN 6.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Bourgeois, NewsNation

item.title: Country singer pauses concert as wife gives birth on tour bus
item.description: Country singer Brantley Gilbert's wife, Amber, welcomed their third child on a tour bus in the middle of his concert on Friday.
item.dc:creator: Christine Samra, WGN 9

item.title: Florida man arrested after allegedly trying to steal generator powering traffic light after hurricane
item.description: A Florida man was arrested on Sunday after he allegedly tried to steal a generator being used to power a traffic light that was likely damaged by Hurricane Milton, authorities say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republican lawsuits target rules for overseas voters, but those ballots are already sent
item.description: Trump and his Republican allies contend these ballots could be part of an elaborate scheme to steal the election from him, a claim for which there is no evidence. Their challenge comes as the voters who receive the ballots are increasingly from groups that are presumed to be Democratic.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: FDA will reconsider decision barring copycat versions of Lilly weight loss drug
item.description: The Food and Drug Administration (FDA) said it will reconsider its decision to remove Eli Lilly’s blockbuster weight loss and diabetes drug from its official shortage list and allow compounding pharmacies to continue selling their own versions.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man armed with rifle accused of threatening FEMA workers in North Carolina
item.description: A man accused of threatening federal disaster workers in western North Carolina has been charged, according to the Rutherford County Sheriff's Office.
item.dc:creator: Michaela Ratliff, KRON4

item.title: Members of the Kennedy family gather for funeral of Ethel Kennedy
item.description: CENTERVILLE, Mass. (AP) — Members of the Kennedy family gathered Monday for the funeral of Ethel Kennedy, the wife of the late Sen. Robert F. Kennedy.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, ABC 4

item.title: FDA shakes up weight loss market as Mounjaro shortage ends
item.description: Federal regulators shook up the booming weight loss drug marketplace when they announced Mounjaro and Zepbound to be no longer in shortage after nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Tom Donohue, former longtime Chamber of Commerce CEO, dies
item.description: Tom Donohue, who led the U.S. Chamber of Commerce for more than two decades before retiring in 2021, has died, the business lobbying giant announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Taylor Giorno, Fox 5

item.title: FEMA temporarily pauses aid in parts of NC over reported threats
item.description: The Federal Emergency Management Agency (FEMA) temporarily paused aid in parts of North Carolina this weekend after alleged threats targeted the agency’s personnel aiding in recovery efforts following Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Lilly Ledbetter, equal pay trailblazer, dies at 86
item.description: Lilly Ledbetter, an activist for equal pay whose legal fight against her employer paved the way for the Fair Pay Act, has died. She was 86.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Group tied to polygamous sect builds fences on US Forest Service land
item.description: A group in Colorado has fenced a parcel of U.S. Forest Service Land in an attempt to claim the land under the Homestead Act of 1862. According to the Montezuma County Sheriff's Office, the actions have resulted in a property dispute between the Free Land Holders and the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Heather Willard, KTLA 5

item.title: Bath & Body Works apologizes for candle designed with what looked like KKK hoods
item.description: Bath & Body Works apologized for selling a winter-themed candle that came under scrutiny for label imagery that drew comparisons to Ku Klux Klan hoods, according to multiple reports.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Massive hole swallows 2 homes in Florida
item.description: The wild scene raised concerns about whether the ground collapse was caused by a sinkhole or water damage from sewage issues.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, ABC 4
